subject: [PATCH 07/13] mm: handle PMD swap entries in fork path

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch improves the handling of PMD (Page Map Directory) swap entries during the fork process in Linux. It introduces a new function `swap_dup_entries_direct` to duplicate a contiguous range of swap slots, and modifies the `copy_huge_non_present_pmd` function to preserve THP (Transparent Huge Pages) by duplicating PMD swap entries instead of splitting them. The patch also adds retry logic for table allocation in case of failure. This change mirrors the existing behavior for PTEs (Page Table Entries).
